title: podman --rm and --detach parameters are not incompatible as man page claims
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

```
# podman run --rm --security-opt label=disable --security-opt seccomp=unconfined --device /dev/fuse:rw --detach --name=buildahctr --net=host -v /var/lib/mycontainer:/var/lib/containers:Z  buildahimage sh -c 'while true ;do wait; done'
```
I expected a warning or an error since I'm running both `--detach` and `--rm` in this command.  The man page states:

```
--rm=true|false

Automatically remove the container when it exits. The default is false.

Note that the container will not be removed when it could not be created or started successfully. This allows the user to inspect the container after failure. The --rm flag is incompatible with the -d flag.
```

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. See descripton

**Describe the results you received:**

The command ran.

**Describe the results you expected:**

An error.  Or if this is meant to work this way, then the man page needs cleansing.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
# podman version
Version:            1.1.2
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.11.5
Git Commit:         a95a49d3038462d033f84ac314ec8a3064a99cff
Built:              Tue Mar  5 13:10:31 2019
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

comments.body: Oh, oops. This one is on me, I must have forgotten to change the manpages.
I've uncommented my description so it can be seen, DOH!
@TomSweeneyRedHat Could you open a PR to fix the man page.
